title: Pandas for Data Analysis: 4 Skills Every Data Scientist Needs
description:
The first real dataset I worked with professionally took me two weeks before I ran a single analysis — not because it was large, but because I didn't understand Pandas properly yet.

This tutorial covers the four skills I return to on every data project: exploring a new dataset in the first 60 seconds, selecting and filtering without the common traps, cleaning messy real-world data without losing row structure, and aggregating with groupby and pivot tables.

Pandas is the most-used library in data science for one reason: every project starts with data that isn't ready. This tutorial teaches you how to handle that.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

This is Tutorial 4 of 10 in the Python for Data Science series. Tutorial 3 covered NumPy — the foundation Pandas is built on. Tutorial 5 covers data visualization.

📋 Free worksheet — Python For Data Science Tutorial 4 Pandas For Data Analysis:
https://worksheets-thebreathnetwork.vercel.app/get-worksheet?slug=python-for-data-science-tutorial-4-pandas-for-data-analysis

If you found this useful, subscribe for weekly tutorials grounded in real data work, not textbook examples.
